channel.title: JLT Pillar Posts
channel.description: In-depth pillar posts from Janine Loves Travel — authority content for JLT agents and travellers

item.title: The personal network every new travel agent begins with is a strong foundation. It is also a finite one.
item.description:
The personal network every new travel agent begins with is a strong foundation. It is also a finite one.
When the first bookings come in through family and old friends, the business is already working. What happens when the messages slow is not a setback. It is a signal that the business is ready to grow outward. 📸
Agents within JLT who move past this stage tend to build in three directions. A consistent public presence draws enquiries from outside the address book, referral habits turn one happy booking into the next, and a clear niche makes an agent easier to find and recommend. 👣
The JLT Academy covers each area directly, including social strategy, niche development, and the habits that keep a travel business moving in slower months. No marketing background required.
Full blog on the website… link in the comments 🔗
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/first-travel-clients-beyond-friends-and-family

item.title: Before you sign anything with a host agency, there are questions worth asking. 📸
item.description:
Before you sign anything with a host agency, there are questions worth asking. 📸
Training is one of the biggest. Ask whether the programme is structured and how long it takes before you are genuinely client-ready. A serious agency will give you a serious answer.
Find out about licensing and protection, too. Does the agency hold an ATOL licence? Are client funds protected? These are not awkward questions. They are sensible ones.
Ask how commission is structured, when it is paid, and what your earning potential looks like. Ask who you call when something goes wrong on a trip. 👣
Ask about contract terms and what happens if you decide to leave. Understand what you are walking into before you commit. 🕶️
The full list of questions is on the blog…
Full blog on the website… link in the comments 🔗
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/questions-to-ask-a-host-agency-before-signing

item.title: Not every "travel agent from home" opportunity is the same thing. 🕶️
item.description:
Not every "travel agent from home" opportunity is the same thing. 🕶️
Some are built around selling holidays. The agent earns from each booking, clean and professional. Others are built around recruitment, where the team you bring in does the heavy lifting on income.
One question tells you which is which. "If I never recruit a single person and only sell holidays, what does my income look like?" A genuine host agency answers that with numbers tied to bookings. If the answer circles back to team-building or downlines, that tells you what the model is built around. 👣
Real UK host agencies sit on supplier relationships, ATOL protection, a proper booking platform, and training that takes months, not days.
Five specific red flags worth knowing on the blog.
Full blog on the website… link in the comments 🔗
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/travel-agent-from-home-mlm-how-to-tell
